description:
A capacity-planning analyst has placed last week’s raw utilisation snapshot in
    /home/user/capacity_data/server_usage.csv.  
    You have to create a concise per-server utilisation report that the analyst
    can drop straight into a slide deck.

    1. Read /home/user/capacity_data/server_usage.csv.  
       • The file is a plain CSV with a **header row** and the following
         columns in this exact order:
           server_id,cpu_usage_pct,mem_usage_pct  
         (The values are already percentages, without the % symbol.)

    2. For every unique server_id, calculate the arithmetic mean of
       cpu_usage_pct and mem_usage_pct.

    3. Save the result to /home/user/capacity_data/usage_summary.log
       using **exactly** the format shown below.  
       • Columns are separated by a single space (ASCII 0x20).  
       • Numbers must be printed with exactly **one digit after
         the decimal point**, even if that digit is zero.  
       • The rows must be sorted by server_id in ascending order.  
       • No header, no extra blank lines.

       <required-format>
       server_id␠avg_cpu_usage_pct␠avg_mem_usage_pct
       s1␠50.0␠66.0
       s2␠70.0␠72.5
       s3␠30.0␠40.0
       </required-format>

       (␠ represents a single literal space; do **not** include the angle
       brackets or the explanatory text.)

    4. When everything is complete, the only visible output in
       /home/user/capacity_data should be:
       • server_usage.csv (unchanged)  
       • usage_summary.log (newly created, exact contents as specified)

    A fast way to achieve this can be done in just 2-3 well-chosen shell
    commands (e.g., awk, sort, redirect), but the choice of tools is up to
    you. The automated tests will read usage_summary.log and verify that it
    matches the required format byte-for-byte, so pay very close attention to
    spacing, number formatting and line order.
truth:
Initial filesystem state:
      Directory: /home/user/capacity_data
      Permissions: 755
      Pre-existing file: /home/user/capacity_data/server_usage.csv
      Permissions: 644
      Contents of server_usage.csv (including final newline):
        server_id,cpu_usage_pct,mem_usage_pct
        s1,45,67
        s1,55,65
        s2,80,70
        s2,60,75
        s3,30,40

    Expected artefact to be created by the agent:
      File: /home/user/capacity_data/usage_summary.log
      Permissions: 644 (agent’s default umask is acceptable)
      Exact contents (including final newline):
        s1 50.0 66.0
        s2 70.0 72.5
        s3 30.0 40.0

    The automated grader will:
      1. Confirm that /home/user/capacity_data/usage_summary.log exists.
      2. md5sum the file and compare against:
           f9739b78645beed62f2a9c9f2a8535f2
      3. Ensure that no additional files were created inside
         /home/user/capacity_data other than usage_summary.log.
